name: Mangrove Crab
description: An important species in mangrove forests, harvested for its rich meat.

name: Sulu Sea Bass
description: Found in the coral reefs of the Sulu Sea, this species thrives in estuaries and coastal waters.

name: Treasure Cucumber
description: Valuable for its use in traditional medicine and as a delicacy.

name: Milkfish
description: Farmed primarily in coastal ponds, this species is significant to both culture and trade.

name: Spanner Crab
description: A rare deep-sea crab, prized for its sweet, tender meat.

name: Sea Turtles
description: These endangered species use the coasts of BARMM for nesting and feeding.

name: Frigate Tuna
description: A smaller tuna species often caught in the Sulu Archipelago, vital for local food security.

name: Sumatran Sailfin Tang
description: An ornamental fish with striking fin patterns, found in the reefs of the Sulu Archipelago.

name: Giant Clams
description: Found in the coral reefs of the Sulu Archipelago, they are iconic symbols of the region’s rich marine biodiversity.

name: Tawi-Tawi Flasher Wrasse
description: Reef wrasse with colorful fins used in display rituals.

name: Sulu Goby
description: Small goby in silty reefs and mangrove lagoons.

name: Sulu Rabbitfish
description: Herbivorous schooling fish in lagoons; algae regulator.

name: Tawi-Tawi Sea Cucumber
description: mercially harvested sea cucumber in sandy areas.

name: Mindanao Clownfish
description: Hardy anemonefish species cohabiting with sea anemones.

name: Basilan Seahorse
description: Mangrove-associated species vulnerable to habitat loss.

name: Philippine Filefish
description: Soft-bodied fish mimicking toxic pufferfish.

name: Sulu Archipelago Wrasse
description: Colorful wrasses contributing to coral cleaning.

name: Tawi-Tawi Fangblenny
description: Small venomous reef fish known for bold behavior.

name: Philippine Longhorn Cowfish
description: Boxfish with horn-like projections; slow-moving reef dweller

name: Basilan Surgeonfish
description: Herbivorous reef fish grazing on algae.

name: Tawi-Tawi Anemonefish
description: Reef clownfish living among host anemones.

name: Sulu Cleaner Shrimp
description: Symbiotic shrimp cleaning parasites off fish.

name: Giant Clam
description: Massive clam contributing to reef structure and filtering.

name: Philippine Sea Krait
description: Sea snake foraging on reef fish; mildly venomous.

name: Sulu Sea Snapper
description: Reef predator forming spawning aggregations.

name: Tawi-Tawi Mantis Shrimp
description: Aggressive shrimp known for its powerful strike.
location: Reef flats and burrows of Tawi-Tawi.

name: Basilan Parrotfish
description: Colorful herbivore grazing on algae and biofilm.

name: Sumatran Sailfin Tang
description: Ornate tang with sail-like fins grazing on algae.

name: Sulu Wrinkled Frogfish
description: Well-camouflaged ambush predator hiding in coral rubble.
location: Coral rubble of the Sulu Archipelago.

name: Tawi-Tawi Leaf Scorpionfish
description: Flat-bodied reef fish mimicking a leaf, waits to ambush prey.

name: Sulu Fairy Basslet
description: Small, colorful reef fish found in large schools.
location: Outer reef slopes of Sulu Archipelago.

name: Tiger-tail Seahorse
commonName: Kabayo-kabayohan na May Puno ng Tigre
description: A black-and-yellow patterned seahorse; often found anchored to corals.
location: Seagrass beds and mangroves of Basilan.

name: Sulu Boxfish
description: Yellow box-shaped reef fish with black spots.

name: Tawi-Tawi Harlequin Sweetlips
description: Juveniles swim with exaggerated motions; adults grow large.
location: Coral slopes near Panglima Sugala, Tawi-Tawi.

name: Philippine Saddleback Anemonefish
description: Anemonefish with a saddle-like white mark.
location: Protected reef lagoons in Sulu and Basilan.

name: Blacklip Butterflyfish
description: Yellow-brown reef fish with vertical black bars.

name: Sulu Marble Shrimp
description: Decorated shrimp with flattened body and camo patterns.
location: Under coral heads and rubble flats.

name: BARMM Bigfin Reef Squid
description: Fast-growing cephalopod, often caught nearshore at night.

name: Philippine Triton Trumpet
description: Large gastropod mollusk that preys on starfish.
location: Reef slopes and sandy bottoms near Tawi-Tawi.
